Specify only critical surfaces for tight tolerance and finish; leave non-functional faces at as-machined condition to reduce finishing and inspection time ($20-50 savings)
risks: Material field is blank; the $0.44 material cost is implausibly low for any metal stock at 54.45 cm3 and suggests a data entry error or system fault that could invalidate the entire quote

103 faces on a 55x55x18 mm envelope is extremely high face count, indicating complex compound surfaces or fine feature detail that 5-axis parametric models frequently underprice due to unplanned tool changes and extended CAM programming time

Setup cost of $200 for a single unit of a high-complexity 5-axis part is likely underestimated; fixturing, datum establishment, and multi-orientation probing on complex geometry routinely runs $400-800 for one-offs
54% material removal with 103 faces suggests deep pockets or undercuts that may require extended reach tooling or custom tooling not captured in the parametric model

Finishing cost of $0.53 is essentially zero for 106 cm2 of surface area on a high-complexity part; any deburring, blending, or specified surface finish requirement will add significant unaccounted labor
No tolerance or surface finish specification is visible in the summary; if tight tolerances exist on any of the 103 faces, machining time could increase 30-50% beyond the parametric estimate
Single unit quantity means no amortization of setup or CAM programming costs, making this quote highly sensitive to any scope creep
